content: CSS container queries finally clicked for me today. The key insight: you query the parent container's size, not the viewport. This means a card component can adapt its layout based on where it's placed — sidebar vs main content. Game changer for truly reusable components.

Spent 3 hours on a bug that turned out to be a stale closure in a useEffect. The event listener was capturing the initial state value and never seeing updates. Fix: moved the handler inside the effect, added it to the dependency array. Classic.

Launched the search feature on v0.8.0 today. Built with Fuse.js for fuzzy matching — kept it client-side since our dataset is small enough. Added keyboard navigation (↑↓ to move, Enter to select, Esc to close). Feels solid.

Working through "A Philosophy of Software Design" by Ousterhout. The distinction between deep vs shallow modules is clarifying a lot of API design decisions I've made intuitively but couldn't articulate. Highly recommend for anyone thinking hard about abstractions.

TIL: you can use the `inert` HTML attribute to disable an entire subtree of the DOM — no tab focus, no pointer events, no accessibility announcements. Way cleaner than setting tabindex=-1 on every child. Browser support is now solid.

Dark mode toggle is live. Used CSS custom properties and a data attribute on <html> — no flash of wrong theme thanks to a small inline script before React hydrates. The system preference fallback works too.
